title: Indie Prototyping, Braid, & Making Innovative Games
creator: Jonathan Blow
description: A pre-release GDC session in which Blow explains how small experimental prototypes led toward Braid and how developers can search for mechanics with unexplored consequences.
useful: It records the design process before Braid became a finished success, making the uncertainty, discarded ideas, and value of exploratory work unusually visible.

manualLinkStatus.note: GDC Vault session page opened successfully during editorial review.

audience: Independent developers and students deciding how to prototype, evaluate an unusual mechanic, and turn experiments into a larger project.
learning: Use prototypes to investigate questions rather than prove a pitch
Recognize when one mechanic supports a wider design space
Compare early design intentions with the finished Braid

editorial.note: Verified against the GDC Vault session record.
